highlights.text: My lean for today was that ES could fill this out to the upside.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean for Monday is that ES can continue building the consolidation which is 6748-50 to 6782-85 with 6765 being a pivot in between.
Institutions buy dips via Failed Breakdowns: They flush a low, grab liquidity underneath, squeeze.

I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “Another option to long is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at 6693.” Early Wednesday morning we flushed 6693 down to 6680, recovered 6693, then went parabolic to 6788 by Thursday evening.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the absolutely textbook Failed Breakdown we had last Wednesday that propelled last weeks 100+ point rush higher.

When ready, next leg up sees 6782-85, 6795, 6807, 6814.” Today we continued to the push, running to 6782-85 then 6795 by the open today, dipping back to that ~6765 pivot, then popping back to 6795 by the close.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled Tight Yet Again. A Move Is Coming. What Way? Oct 7th Plan
